QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $708.93 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: US equities fell hard on Friday's session as the Trump-Xi summit concluded without any chip trade breakthrough and oil surged above $105 on escalating Hormuz disruption. The S&P 500 dropped 1.1% to 739.17, the QQQ (with a noted data anomaly) lost 1.5% to 708.93, and the SOXX semiconductor index sank 3.9% to 508.52. The 10-year Treasury yield jumped 7bp to 4.59% as markets digested a hot CPI and hawkish comments from incoming Fed Chair Warsh.
commodities_note: WTI crude oil surged 2.98% to $105.42 as Hormuz ship seizures intensified and Trump rejected Iran's ceasefire proposal. Gold eased 1.39% to $4,555.80. The DXY dollar index firmed to 99.27 (+0.23%). QQQ data shows a possible contract or source anomaly — price $708.93 is above historical norms; use 5d/1mo changes as the more reliable trend indicators.

thesis_highlights: WeRide Q1 2026 results (reported May 13 2026): revenue +58% YoY on Robotaxi fleet expansion and L2++ ADAS commercialisation — cite the print specifics when news references it.
WeRide bull case rests on B2B fleet licensing (Uber, Grab, Bosch) + multi-country Robotaxi deployment — evaluate on commercial traction, not autonomy benchmarks vs Waymo/Tesla.
ADR/China delisting risk is a standing consideration — always maintain starter sizing for WRD.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain CAUTION as NIM compression persists with US10Y at 4.59%, well above the 4.20% relief threshold, and MAS S$NEER appreciation still in place until October 2026. DBS is the most extended with RSI 81, while UOB has a slightly cleaner technical profile but still an RSI block.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — no NIM relief until at least October 2026 MAS decision.
singapore.key_development: No change in fundamentals; elevated US yields and MAS policy keep the dual headwind intact. Re-entry zones are well below current prices.

semiconductors.summary: The semiconductor cluster sold off sharply (SOXX -3.9% to 508.52) after the Trump-Xi summit failed to produce a chip deal. All members are CAUTION, with many at extreme extensions >20% above their 50-day SMAs. The broad pullback hasn't yet provided actionable entries; NVDA earnings on May 21 and Samsung's threatened strike on May 21 are the immediate catalysts.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically overextended — sector-wide correction needed before entries re-open.
semiconductors.key_development: No chip breakthrough in Beijing talks; TSM share sale added pressure; memory names like MU and SK Hynix remain extremely elevated. LITE and NVDA photonics are the brightest spots fundamentally but technically blocked.

big_tech.summary: BigTech names all remain CAUTION. MSFT gave back some of the Ackman-fueled gains but is the least extended at +5.7%; AMZN and GOOG are >12-19% above their SMAs. Google I/O next week could shift the narrative, but for now, entry is blocked by overextension.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — MSFT's AI momentum is intact but OpenAI concentration risk persists; AMZN/GOOG need pullbacks to reset.
big_tech.key_development: Google I/O (week of May 20) is the next major catalyst; if TPU v7 AI infrastructure deals are announced, GOOG could become the most attractive post-pullback. PLTR remains a high-PE outlier below its 50-day.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM3E supplier and largest memory producer; its threatened strike starting May 21 directly tightens HBM supply, benefiting SK Hynix and Micron.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung strike disrupts production >5 days, spiking HBM ASPs further.

interconnected.name: Cadence Design Systems
interconnected.reason: EDI software leader directly impacted by US export controls to China — read-through to ASML and INTC decoupling thesis.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit if new US chip export restrictions on EDA tools are formally announced.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking switch leader; benefits from NVIDIA Spectrum-X adoption and hyperscaler capex, linking to NOK's optical networking thesis.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist on pullback to $X if hyperscaler switch orders accelerate post-NVDA earnings.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Sister NVDA $2B photonics investment alongside LITE; co-packaged optics supply chain twin, directly tied to LITE and NVDA optical interconnect demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor for a pullback below its 50-day; add if NVDA confirms photonics shipment milestones.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and datacenter networking silicon provider; benefits from the same AI infrastructure buildout as AVGO and NVDA, with exposure to hyperscaler custom chip designs.
interconnected.entry_note: Consider adding if AVGO earnings (June 4) confirm custom ASIC pipeline strength, driving sector-wide re-rating.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz oil blockade: Strait of Hormuz shipping severely disrupted since March; WTI surged to $105.42 as US-Iran talks stall and new ship seizures escalate.
US-China semiconductor export controls: Summit failed to produce chip deal; H200 sales to Chinese firms approved but not yet shipped; broader technology tensions persist.
Fed hawkish pivot under Warsh: Hot 3.8% CPI, rising yields (US10Y 4.59%), and hawkish comments from Schmid and Collins raise probability of rate hikes, pressuring growth stocks.
Samsung labor strike (May 21–June 7): Up to 45,000 workers threaten walkout; could disrupt global memory chip supply, benefiting SK Hynix/Micron but adding uncertainty.
geopolitical.market_impact: Semis sold off 3.9% as US-China chip hopes faded and yields surged. Singapore banks remain under NIM compression with no relief in sight. Oil-sensitive sectors face input cost pressure; defense stocks like PLTR have a tailwind but are overvalued.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Samsung union confirms strike plan for May 21 despite company's pay talks offer (per Reuters, May 15). NVIDIA H200 China approvals reported but chip export controls were 'not a major topic' at summit (per Reuters).
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries; wait for VIX to settle below 18, semis to correct 10-15% from extremes, and earnings binary events to clear.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — extreme extension across semis and banks, no entries.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Twenty-one of 22 names are hard-blocked on technical extension or in valuation caution; only WRD offers a WATCH setup, but ADR/delisting fragility caps size. NVDA earnings on May 21 and the Samsung strike are the week's binary risks.

watch_for_entry.note: R:R 4.1:1 to $8.33 with invalidation at $6.85. B2B fleet licensing multi-country rollout is the thesis; ADR risk keeps sizing to starter. Wait for two-day hold above $7.14.
